name: Adolf Frey's Pike Quenelles
recipeInstructions:
1. To make quenelles, grind pike in a food processor or sift through a tamis. Set aside.
2. Bring water to a boil. Sift flour into the boiling water and stir until absorbed. Keep stirring to prevent sticking. Remove from heat and beat in 1 egg. Cool mixture, then refrigerate until cold. Remove from refrigerator and blend with pike in a food processor until smooth. This is the panade.
3. Cream butter in a small bowl. Put ground pike into a bowl set inside another bowl of ice. Season with salt and pepper, mix in panade, and gradually add other whole eggs and egg yolks. Blend well, then add butter. Chill mixture for 30 minutes.
4. To make white sauce, simmer chicken stock in a heavy saucepan until reduced to about 1 cup liquid (approximately 45 minutes). Whisk thickened cream into the stock, adjusting consistency with more or less cream as desired. Season with salt and pepper. Strain if desired.
5. Shape quenelles by mounding mixture between two warmed spoons. Place each quenelle on a floured surface.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F. Butter a large baking dish. In a wide saucepan, bring 3 quarts water to a boil and poach quenelles for 15 minutes without letting the water boil. Drain thoroughly.
7. Arrange quenelles in the prepared baking dish. Nip each quenelle with a fork before placing it in the dish. Pour enough white sauce over the quenelles to cover, then sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
8. Bake for 15-20 minutes until the cheese browns. Serve 2-3 quenelles per diner.
